channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: Critically appraised paper: Mobile-based pulmonary rehabilitation improved exercise capacity as much as and health status more than centre-based pulmonary rehabilitation for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease [commentary].
item.description: A critically appraised paper indicates that mobile-based pulmonary rehabilitation improved exercise capacity as much as and health status more than centre-based pulmonary rehabilitation for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Do blood flow restriction exercises offer additional benefits when compared to conventional exercises in musculoskeletal rehabilitation? A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of RCTs found that low-load exercise with blood flow restriction (LLE-BFR) offers additional benefits over low-load exercise (LLE) and high-load exercise (HLE) in improving muscle strength, disability, and pain intensity for individuals with musculoskeletal conditions.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Frontline agreement in diagnosis and treatment for musculoskeletal dysfunctions. A prospective, randomized, controlled study.
item.description: This randomized controlled trial found high agreement in diagnosis, treatment, and discharge decisions between physiotherapists and resident physicians compared to senior physicians for musculoskeletal dysfunctions in an emergency department setting.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Development of a Core Patient-Reported Outcome (Measures) Set for Pediatric Physical Therapy.
item.description: This study developed a core set of nine patient-reported outcomes (PROs) and corresponding measures for pediatric physical therapy, suitable for daily practice, research, and quality assessment, grouped into functioning, health, and well-being domains.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Critically appraised papers: Adding physiotherapy or motivational text messages does not improve pain beyond home exercise alone for degenerative meniscal tear and knee pain [synopsis].
item.description: For degenerative meniscal tear and knee pain, adding physiotherapy or motivational text messages did not improve pain beyond home exercise alone, according to a critically appraised paper.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Recent highlights in motor recovery and rehabilitation research after stroke.
item.description: A systematic search of 118 RCTs, RCT protocols, and systematic reviews summarised recent advances in motor recovery and rehabilitation research after stroke since 2020 and provided recommendations for future trials.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Smallest worthwhile effect of anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction compared with non-surgical management: a benefit-harm trade-off study.
item.description: A benefit-harm trade-off study in 92 recreational or competitive athletes estimated the smallest worthwhile effect of ACL reconstruction plus rehabilitation versus rehabilitation alone for improving knee function and return to sport.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Psychometric Properties of the Trunk Impairment Scale Used After Stroke: A Systematic Review Using COSMIN Methodology.
item.description: A systematic review using COSMIN methodology evaluated the measurement properties of the Trunk Impairment Scale (TIS) in individuals with stroke, finding that while widely used, the quality and certainty of evidence regarding its psychometric properties have been unclear.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Two-Year Outcomes of the Proximal Hamstring Avulsion Rehabilitation Regimes: Longitudinal Versus Accelerated Protocol (PHARRLAP) Study: A Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial with 60 patients undergoing proximal hamstring tendon repair for acute avulsions found that a conservative rehabilitation (CR) pathway and an accelerated rehabilitation (AR) pathway yielded comparable 2-year clinical outcomes, strength recovery, functional symmetry, and complication/re-injury rates.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Improved Flexor Spasticity in Patients with Chronic Stroke Using a Dynamic Wrist-Hand Stretching Device: A Pilot Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: A pilot randomized controlled trial with 30 chronic stroke patients found that a novel dynamic wrist-hand stretching device significantly improved flexor spasticity and hand function compared to a control group.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Efficacy and Modulating Factors of Repetitive Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation on Motor Function in Parkinson's Disease: A Meta-Analysis.
item.description: This meta-analysis evaluated the short-term and long-term therapeutic effects of repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS) on motor function in Parkinson's disease (PD) and identified key modulating factors like stimulation site and frequency.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Establishing weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury: a systematic review and methodological quality analysis.
item.description: This systematic review established weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury, highlighting the current lack of consensus and the need for standardized, multidimensional assessments to reduce recurrence rates.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Shortwave diathermy for managing knee osteoarthritis: A systematic review with meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of 17 trials (N=1,372) found that shortwave diathermy (SWD) significantly improved pain (mean -2.13; 95% CI [-2.64, -1.62]; p < .01) and walking ability (mean -0.72; 95% CI [-1.08, -0.37]; p < .01) in patients with knee osteoarthritis.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Patterns of Outcome Measure Utilization in Stroke Rehabilitation Lower Extremity Trials Conducted in Low-to-Middle versus High-Income Countries.
item.description: A systematic review of 1548 stroke lower extremity motor rehabilitation RCTs found that low- to middle-income countries used more generic impairment and activity of daily living outcome measures relying on observation rather than direct patient involvement compared with high-income countries.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Anti-Compensatory Saccades Changes After Visuo-Vestibular Physical Therapy in People With Acute Unilateral Vestibulopathy: A Prospective Observational Study.
item.description: In a prospective observational study of 92 people with acute unilateral vestibulopathy, anti-compensatory saccades assessed by SHIMP showed significant improvement after 8 weeks of visuo-vestibular physical therapy.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Prioritising fall prevention strategies in Parkinson's disease: A multi-method evaluation.
item.description: A multi-method evaluation integrating a narrative review, Delphi expert consensus with 15 multidisciplinary experts, and a best-worst method quantitatively prioritised fall prevention strategies in Parkinson's disease.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Associations of neuropathic pain and pain catastrophizing with rehabilitation outcomes in patients with stroke.
item.description: A prospective observational study found that neuropathic pain and pain catastrophizing were associated with poorer Barthel Index, Brunnstrom staging, and quality of life scores in inpatients with stroke undergoing rehabilitation, though only pain catastrophizing remained independently associated with some outcomes after adjusting for confounders.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Multi-Domain Benefits of Exergaming for Sarcopenia Management in Institutionalized Older Adults: Physical, Psychological, and Social Outcomes.
item.description: A 12-week exergaming intervention improved physical, psychological, and social outcomes in 72 institutionalized older adults with sarcopenia, including a 12.3% increase in knee extensor strength and an 18.5% increase in 6MWT.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Sarcopenia Increases Adjacent Segment Degeneration Risk within 3 Years of Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion.
item.description: A retrospective cohort study of 333 adult patients undergoing elective ACDF found that sarcopenia increases the risk of radiographic adjacent segment degeneration development within 3 years of index surgery.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Role of Platelet-Rich Plasma Injection in Anterior Cruciate Ligament Reconstruction: A Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials
item.description: A meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials investigated the role of platelet-rich plasma injection in anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction.
item.author: PubMed
